message:
feat(search_bridge): Cx-first BridgeCancellationToken::cancelled_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close search_bridge.rs's last cx gap on BridgeCancellationToken.

`cancelled_with_cx(cx)` returns a new `BridgeWaitOutcome` enum that
reports which of two signals fired first:
- `BridgeCancelled` — the bridge's own cancel token was triggered.
- `CxCancelled` — the caller's cx was cancelled first.

Fast paths avoid the select race when either side is already
cancelled at entry. The race itself uses `runtime_compat::select!`
to multiplex the bridge's `Notify::notified()` against a cx-aware
yield loop that polls `cx.checkpoint()` at tick boundaries.

This lets supervisor futures short-circuit a bridge-cancel wait when
an outer shutdown scope cancels, without depending on the bridge's
own notify path (which may be driven by a search task that itself
needs cancellation).

Legacy `cancelled()` preserved unchanged.

Two regression tests pin the contract:
- `cancelled_with_cx_observes_bridge_cancel_fast_path` — pre-cancelled
  bridge + fresh cx returns `BridgeCancelled`.
- `cancelled_with_cx_observes_cx_cancel_when_bridge_live` —
  pre-cancelled cx + live bridge returns `CxCancelled` AND the
  bridge token is NOT inadvertently cancelled (observability
  invariant — cx-cancel must not mutate bridge state).

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 57/57
search_bridge tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
